criteria.text: Inclusion: HFrEF with LVEF ≤35% and Loeys-Dietz syndrome with aortic root dilation; age 50-75; able to participate in GDMT titration protocol. Exclusion: prior aortic dissection, pregnancy, advanced renal dysfunction (creatinine >2.0 mg/dL) or hypotension precluding GDMT.
Female, age 50-75, breast cancer with radiographically evident lymphangitic involvement; presenting with severe hypoxia; not enrolled in other trials.
Inclusion: Idiopathic dilated cardiomyopathy with EF ~15%, presence of left ventricular thrombus; no contraindication to GDMT or anticoagulation; adequate renal function for planned therapies.
Metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer with spinal cord compression; suitable for palliative External Beam Radiotherapy (EBRT) and systemic AR-targeted therapy if tolerated.
N/A; not enrolled in any trial.

Eligible: adult ICU admission with hypovolemic shock due to severe secretory diarrhea; no contraindication to aggressive fluid resuscitation; no advanced directives restricting life-sustaining interventions.
Not enrolled in any clinical trial; ICU admission for hyperosmolar hyperglycemic state with dehydration and plasma hyperosmolality.
Meets criteria for standard oncodology management of fallopian tube carcinoma with hydrops tubae profluens triad; not enrolled in a clinical trial.
WPW syndrome with orthodromic AVRT; no exclusion criteria identified for this cardiovascular trajectory.

Adult male with suspected SVC syndrome due to calcified mediastinal histoplasmosis lymphadenopathy; no contraindication to antifungal therapy; suitable for endovascular intervention if needed; agrees to follow-up.
Acute AVNRT with adenosine termination; adult female patient; two-encounter trajectory with ED presentation and discharge.

Eligible for thrombolysis given prosthetic mitral valve thrombosis with hemodynamic stability; no active major bleeding; no recent intracranial hemorrhage; age > 50; plan for short-term invasive therapy if thrombolysis fails.
Inclusion: Age ≥ 50 with high-output heart failure secondary to Paget's disease; Exclusion: pregnancy, acute coronary syndrome, severe hepatic failure.
ICU admission for acute hyponatremia due to water intoxication with status epilepticus; no contraindication to hypertonic saline or standard antiseizure therapy.

Eligible: radiographically and cytologically confirmed malignant pericardial effusion related to non-small cell lung adenocarcinoma; not enrolled in conflicting trials; able to provide informed consent.

Inclusion: post-MI Dressler syndrome presenting with pleuritic chest pain within 2-6 weeks after myocardial infarction; inflammatory markers elevated; hemodynamically stable. Exclusion: signs of tamponade, active infection, contraindication to NSAIDs.
Eligible: exertional angina with no prior MI; no contraindication to beta-blocker or nitroglycerin therapy; consent obtained for outpatient follow-up.
Inclusion: adolescent male with exertional syncope and suspected CPVT; Exclusion: structural heart disease or long QT syndrome evident on baseline testing.
Not enrolled in any trial; clinical scenario validated for radiation-induced valvular disease with multi-valvular stenosis and calcification.
Synthetic patient; meets scenario of hepatocellular carcinoma with post-embolization syndrome after TACE; no trial enrollment.

Adult patient with HTLV-1 associated ATLL presenting with lytic bone lesions. Includes baseline HTLV-1 positivity, measurable disease, and no contraindication to zidovudine/interferon therapy. Excludes patients with significant uncontrolled infections or prior intensive chemotherapy for ATLL.
SVCS due to calcified mediastinal histoplasmosis lymphadenopathy; adult male, baseline evaluation completed; status to monitor for potential venous stenting.
Meets high-risk essential thrombocythemia criteria (age >60 and prior thrombotic event) with recurrent digital ischemia and TIAs; candidate for cytoreductive therapy and antiplatelet management.
Congenital Long QT Syndrome Type 2; recurrent syncope with episodes concerning torsades de pointes; genetic testing planned; no contraindications to beta-blocker therapy.
Cardiovascular surgical case; no trial eligibility; postoperative complication (chylopericardium) following thoracic aortic aneurysm repair.
Stage IV gallbladder cancer with porta hepatis invasion; eligible for systemic chemotherapy with gemcitabine/cisplatin; biliary obstruction requiring palliation with ERCP/stent.

Inclusion: Female, age 50-65, locally advanced inflammatory breast cancer (IBC) with dermal lymphatic invasion; upper-extremity lymphedema; candidate for neoadjuvant chemotherapy and planned mastectomy with axillary staging and radiotherapy; Exclusion: active infection, pregnancy, uncontrolled comorbidity.

Inclusion: symptomatic severe calcific aortic stenosis; candidate for TAVR after heart-team evaluation. Exclusion: active infection, hemodynamic instability, active endocarditis, pregnancy not applicable.
Eligible for diagnostic CMD evaluation; not enrolled in active clinical trial; nonpregnant; no contraindication to acetylcholine testing.
Inclusion: NSTEMI with ischemic mitral regurgitation and cardiogenic shock; Exclusion: irreversible multi-organ failure or contraindication to antiplatelets.
Eligible for pembrolizumab + lenvatinib combination therapy for metastatic endometrial carcinoma; adequate organ function; no active autoimmune disease; not pregnant.

Inclusion: newly diagnosed Chronic Myeloid Leukemia presenting in blast crisis with hyperleukocytosis and leukostasis; Exclusion: active uncontrolled infection or end-stage organ failure that would preclude cytoreductive therapy.

ICU-level thyroid storm triggered by acute infection; meets criteria for critical care monitoring and aggressive thyroiditis management. Not pregnant. Prior cardiovascular disease not prohibitive for therapy.

Postpartum female with spontaneous coronary artery dissection (SCAD) of the LAD presenting with acute chest pain and elevated troponins; postpartum state within 6 weeks; no contraindication to conservative ACS management.
Inclusion: Adult with decompensated cirrhosis and suspected spontaneous bacterial peritonitis (SBP); Exclusion: pregnancy, age <18, ceftriaxone allergy, or withdrawal of care.

Inclusion: age ≥18; histologically or radiographically confirmed hilar cholangiocarcinoma (Klatskin tumor) with radiographic evidence of intrahepatic biliary dilation; ECOG 0-2; adequate hepatic function after biliary drainage (bilirubin <6 mg/dL). Exclusion: uncontrolled infection, significant comorbidity precluding chemotherapy.

Inclusion: male, age 60-80, newly diagnosed penile squamous cell carcinoma with chronic ulcer and inguinal lymphadenopathy; scheduled for diagnostic biopsy and potential surgical management. Exclusion: prior pelvic radiotherapy, distant metastasis, or prior systemic therapy for penile cancer.
Eligible for ischemic cardiomyopathy viability MRI study; meets criteria for viability assessment; variation seed 1162648548.

Inclusion: age 60-75 with acute purulent pericarditis secondary to pneumococcal empyema; hemodynamically stable at baseline. Exclusion: pregnancy, immunosuppression with neutropenia, life expectancy <30 days.

Inclusion: age 18-65; presenting with severe traumatic brain injury (GCS <= 8) and radiologic evidence of intracranial injury; candidate for intracranial pressure (ICP) monitoring. Exclusion: pre-existing advanced neurodegenerative disease, do-not-resuscitate in place, hemorrhagic diathesis precluding invasive monitoring.
Meets eligibility for management of hypercalcemia of malignancy in known lung squamous cell carcinoma; not enrolled in a trial.

Eligible: CTO of circumflex artery with viable collateralized lateral ischemia; Excluded: acute myocardial infarction within 48 hours or recent revascularization in prior 30 days, severe LV dysfunction (LVEF < 30%), contrast allergy, eGFR < 30 mL/min/1.73m2.

Pediatric patient with history of Kawasaki disease and imaging-confirmed coronary artery aneurysm; clinically stable; eligible for conservative antithrombotic management with aspirin +/- clopidogrel; no active bleeding or renal/hepatic dysfunction.

Metastatic renal cell carcinoma on first-line sunitinib therapy; presenting with Grade 3 palmar-plantar erythrodysesthesia; not enrolled in a cancer trial; deemed suitable for dose modification and symptom management.
High-risk essential thrombocythemia with thrombotic events; meets criteria for cytoreductive therapy; variation seed 3037322727
Traumatic thoracic aortic injury; candidate for TEVAR; admitted to ICU for hemodynamic stabilization; no contraindication to endovascular repair.

Metastatic breast cancer with liver involvement presenting with acute hepatic sinusoidal obstruction syndrome (HVOD) following chemotherapy; requiring supportive management and treatment modification.

Male, 62 years old with cirrhosis and ascites; diagnosed with Hepatorenal Syndrome Type 1; admitted to ICU; currently refractory to maximum-dose midodrine, octreotide, and albumin therapy; escalation to vasopressor therapy pursued; candidate for liver transplant evaluation.

Adult female with suspected fibromuscular dysplasia causing renal artery stenosis presenting with refractory hypertension; criteria met for evaluation and potential angioplasty; exclude pregnancy and known contraindications to endovascular intervention.

Meets inclusion: metastatic gastric adenocarcinoma with upper GI bleed; iron deficiency anemia; suitable for short-term supportive care with potential consideration of systemic therapy after stabilization.
Male, age 60, presenting with Leriche syndrome features (claudication, impotence, diminished pulses); evaluated for elective vascular revascularization.
CTO of circumflex artery scheduled for high-risk wiring PCI; eligible for dual antiplatelet therapy and PCI with no contraindications.
Not enrolled in a clinical trial. Acute digoxin toxicity presenting with bidirectional VT.
Adult female with malignancy-associated TLS; meets criteria for severe TLS with hyperkalemia and acute uric acid nephropathy requiring ICU-level care.
Inclusion: female age 50-60 with new nipple changes and imaging suggesting underlying breast malignancy; Exclusion: pregnancy, prior breast cancer, active infection.
Ischemic cardiomyopathy with post-infarction ventricular septal rupture; candidate for percutaneous VSR closure; no active infection, adequate frailty status, expected survival >12 months.

Adult with acute myocarditis presenting with ventricular arrhythmias and rapid heart failure progression; no significant chronic kidney disease; no contraindication to short-term high-dose steroids; no active systemic infection.
Meets ARDS criteria with viral pneumonia; admitted to ICU; candidate for prone positioning; no contraindication to proning; surrogate consent obtained for ICU interventions
Inclusion: Acute anterior MI with evolving conduction block; 3-encounter trajectory; Exclusion: none significant.

Meets criteria for evaluation of exertional chest pain with suspected myocardial bridging of ramus intermedius; no prior PCI; no known acute coronary syndrome; provided consent for observational management and imaging.

Inclusion: adult with suspected acute mesenteric ischemia presenting with disproportionately severe abdominal pain and metabolic acidosis; Exclusion: pregnancy, incarceration, or non-ischemic abdominal pain.
Ischemic cardiomyopathy with suspected hibernating myocardium; viability study (Cardiac MRI) planned and completed to guide revascularization decisions.

Inclusion: Acute rheumatic mitral stenosis with severe pulmonary hypertension and atrial fibrillation; Exclusion: contraindication to anticoagulation or to percutaneous balloon mitral valvotomy (BMV).
Not applicable; synthetic trajectory for cardiovascular poisoning case.
Cardiovascular postoperative chylopericardium due to thoracic duct injury during CABG; 3-encounter trajectory.

Inclusion: Adult with severe traumatic brain injury and intracranial hypertension requiring hyperosmolar therapy and ICP monitoring. Exclusion: Active intracranial hemorrhage contraindicating invasive monitoring or hypertonic saline therapy at presentation; known drug allergies to study medications.
Age 50-65; Male; Raynaud phenomenon with digital ulcers; secondary Raynaud phenomenon suspected; not enrolled in any clinical trial; no contraindication to vasodilator therapy.

Inclusion: symptomatic severe calcific aortic stenosis with plan for transcatheter valve replacement (TAVR). Exclusion: active infection, prohibitive vascular access issues, or end-stage kidney disease.
Inclusion: NSTEMI with cardiogenic shock requiring mechanical circulatory support; Exclusion: active major bleeding or terminal illness; Eligible for invasive strategy with mechanical support.
MINOCA phenotype with microvascular angina and patent epicardial arteries on angiography; meets age and presentation criteria for the cardiovascular MINOCA trajectory.

Adult female with congenital Ebstein's anomaly presenting with severe tricuspid regurgitation and accessory pathway; eligible for electrophysiology evaluation and potential surgical repair with pathway ablation.
Cardiovascular surgery patient; chylopericardium suspected post CABG; age 55-65; consented; no prior thoracic duct disorder.

Eligible for immune checkpoint inhibitor therapy; developed immune-mediated pneumonitis suspected to be related to pembrolizumab; appropriate for glucocorticoid therapy with plan for close oncology follow-up and possible resumption of pembrolizumab after pneumonitis resolution.
Inclusion: Age >=18 with acute decompensated heart failure precipitated by medication and dietary nonadherence; Exclusion: none.

Advanced epithelial ovarian cancer with massive ascites and bowel sub-occlusion; cachexia; eligible for palliative systemic therapy; no active infection or uncontrolled organ failure; not enrolled in conflicting trial.
Meets criteria for dilated cardiomyopathy with alcohol etiology; age 52 male; no contraindication to guideline-directed medical therapy at baseline.
Inclusion: Female, age 30-45 with suspected renovascular hypertension due to fibromuscular dysplasia; Exclusion: pregnancy, eGFR < 30 mL/min/1.73m2, alternative secondary causes of HTN.
Eligible for acute coronary syndrome management with surgical repair for papillary muscle rupture; no prior prosthetic mitral valve; informed consent obtained.

Inclusion: Postmenopausal female with acute chest pain after emotional stress; imaging shows apical ballooning; coronary angiography demonstrates no obstructive coronary disease; compatible with Takotsubo cardiomyopathy.
Eligible for acute limb-threatening DVT intervention; cardiovascular risk profile supports intervention.
Inclusion: Adult with congenital Ebstein's anomaly; Exclusion: acute coronary syndrome, infectious endocarditis, unrepaired complete heart block.

Inclusion: acute phlegmasia cerulea dolens of the right lower extremity with extensive iliofemoral DVT; Exclusion: active major bleeding, known contraindication to anticoagulation or thrombolysis, uncorrected coagulopathy.
Marfan syndrome with progressive aortic root dilation; candidate for ongoing medical management with imaging surveillance; excludes emergent surgical intervention at baseline.
Inclusion: symptomatic severe rheumatic mitral stenosis with atrial fibrillation and pulmonary hypertension; Exclusion: pregnancy, prior mitral valve replacement, contraindication to anticoagulation.

Meets criteria for gynecologic oncology evaluation: postmenopausal status, pelvic mass >10 cm, histology suspicious for uterine sarcoma; adequate organ function; no distant metastasis on baseline imaging.
Inclusion: Chronic total occlusion of RCA with viable myocardium; candidate for high-risk CTO-PCI; coronary anatomy suitable for PCI. Exclusion: hemodynamic instability or recent STEMI.
ACS with high-degree AV block; inpatient management with temporary pacing and PCI planned; not enrolled in any trial.
Not enrolled in any trial.
Eligible: adults undergoing cardiac surgery with risk for thoracic duct injury and potential chylopericardium; exclusions: none
Acute Stanford type A aortic dissection presenting with acute tamponade and malperfusion to right carotid; requires emergent surgical repair.
Acute Sinus of Valsalva aneurysm rupture with RVOT fistula; age 60; suitable anatomy for emergent open surgical repair; no contraindications identified.

Inclusion: adult (age ≥18) with newly diagnosed mid-esophageal squamous cell carcinoma; progressive dysphagia; significant weight loss; ECOG 0-1; adequate organ function. Exclusion: distant metastases at presentation; uncontrolled comorbidity.

Breast cancer with Paget's disease; confirmed invasive ductal carcinoma; suitable for breast-conserving surgery with sentinel lymph node biopsy and adjuvant therapy; not enrolled in a concurrent trial.
Ischemic mitral regurgitation secondary to LV remodeling after lateral wall MI; male aged 60-65 included. Excludes non-ischemic MR, prior mitral valve replacement, or severe CKD (eGFR <30).
64-year-old male with exertional dyspnea and strongly positive stress test; diagnosed with left main coronary artery disease with multivessel CAD; candidate for surgical revascularization (CABG).
Stage III HER2-positive breast cancer; planned neoadjuvant AC followed by taxane + trastuzumab; no active pregnancy; baseline LVEF adequate; no significant hepatic/renal impairment.
Not applicable; clinical trajectory focused on congenital LQTS2 with TdP risk; genetic testing and family screening planned.
Trial eligibility constraints: ICU synthetic data; not enrolled in any trial.

Male, 68 with ischemic cardiomyopathy and CRT-D in place. VT storm requiring inpatient antiarrhythmic therapy and device interrogation. Exclusions: pregnancy is not applicable; no known severe hepatic dysfunction; no contraindication to amiodarone.
Meets ACS pathway criteria; no contraindications to dual antiplatelet therapy; not enrolled in other trials.
Pediatric patient (late adolescence) with idiopathic biventricular cardiomyopathy transitioning toward Adult Congenital Heart Disease (ACHD) care; eligible for GDMT with titration.
Not enrolled in any trial; emergent massive air embolism due to central venous catheter complication.
Acute coronary syndrome with cardiogenic shock following CABG; VA-ECMO candidate; not pregnant; expected ICU course >48 hours; no contraindication to mechanical circulatory support.

Male, age 58, with known cirrhosis and ascites presenting with acute kidney injury consistent with HRS-AKI; currently on standard vasoconstrictor therapy (midodrine, octreotide) with intravenous albumin, but with inadequate renal response.

Meets criteria for AML induction therapy (7+3) with no prior cytotoxic therapy; informed consent obtained; baseline organ function adequate for intensive chemotherapy with close infectious risk monitoring.

Inclusion: Rapidly progressive anaplastic thyroid carcinoma with airway compromise; eligible for emergent airway stabilization with palliative intent. Exclusion: plan for curative surgery unlikely due to aggressive tumor biology.
Unrepaired congenital aortic coarctation presenting with severe upper-extremity hypertension; eligible for rapid evaluation and potential endovascular repair.

Inclusion: diagnosis of ileal neuroendocrine tumor with carcinoid syndrome; age 18-85; adequate hepatic/renal function; no prior octreotide intolerance. Exclusion: pregnancy or lactation; uncontrolled comorbities precluding study participation.
Not enrolled in any cardiovascular trial; eligible for non-invasive imaging. Clinically stable after baseline workup; persistent Kawasaki-related coronary aneurysm diagnosed via imaging.
Meets trajectory criteria for cardiovascular RV failure due to severe, untreated obstructive sleep apnea; not enrolled in any concurrent trial.
Eligible for surgical evaluation; not enrolled in conflicting trials; suitable for neoadjuvant therapy consideration.

High CHA2DS2-VASc score (≥6) with new-onset non-valvular atrial fibrillation presenting with rapid ventricular response; DOAC-based anticoagulation considered; no contraindications identified; Variation seed: 1530910410.

NSCLC: adenocarcinoma with hypertrophic pulmonary osteoarthropathy. Variation seed: 776945201. Inclusion: no prior systemic therapy for lung cancer; planned first-line platinum-doublet. Exclusion: active secondary malignancy or significant comorbidity precluding chemotherapy.

ICU admission for acute hypercalcemic crisis due to primary hyperparathyroidism with associated pancreatitis; delirium and dehydration present; require IV hydration and calcium-lowering therapy; eligible for synthetic trajectory
Not enrolled in any interventional cancer trial; age 50-65; fit for surgical management; no known contraindication to anesthesia; LMS suspected, biopsy planned to confirm diagnosis.
Inclusion: Female 18-40 with gestational trophoblastic disease; Exclusion: prior GTN chemotherapy; Not currently pregnant; Adequate organ function for EMA-CO.
Not enrolled in any clinical trial; Presented with inferior STEMI and treated with urgent PCI with drug-eluting stent (DES) to Right Coronary Artery (RCA).
Inclusion: adult male with 60% TBSA thermal burns admitted to ICU for burn shock resuscitation; Exclusion: none identified at baseline

Inclusion: Age > 18 with suspected acute mesenteric ischemia and septic peritonitis requiring emergent surgery; Exclusion: Do-not-resuscitate status or comfort-measures-only; terminal illness limiting aggressive care.

Simulated ICU envenomation trajectory for pit viper with venom-induced consumptive coagulopathy; meets criteria for ICU monitoring and antivenom therapy; no pregnancy; no chronic anticoagulation; allows 2-4 encounter course.
Inclusion: adult with suspected severe aplastic anemia requiring immunosuppressive therapy; Exclusion: active uncontrolled infection contraindicating ATG at baseline.
Not enrolling in a trial; meets criteria for thoracic oncology evaluation; 35 pack-year smoking history.

ECOG 0-1; histologically confirmed metastatic endometrial adenocarcinoma; prior platinum-based therapy; adequate organ function; no active autoimmune disease requiring systemic therapy; not pregnant or breastfeeding.
New-onset non-valvular atrial fibrillation with rapid ventricular response; CHA2DS2-VASc score 4; candidate for DOAC anticoagulation; no contraindications identified.
Eligible for post-cardiac arrest care protocol; no contraindications identified; consent obtained for standard ACS workup.

Meets criteria for transcatheter edge-to-edge repair of severe functional tricuspid regurgitation; preserved LV systolic function; no prohibitive comorbidity; able to tolerate TEER and post-procedure diuresis.

Eligible for pembrolizumab + lenvatinib combination therapy for metastatic endometrial adenocarcinoma; ECOG 0-1; no active autoimmune disease requiring systemic immunosuppression; adequate organ function.

Inclusion: age ≥18, new diagnosis of suspected upper tract urothelial carcinoma with gross hematuria. Exclusion: active infection, pregnancy, known allergy to iodinated contrast, ongoing participation in another interventional trial.
Inclusion: symptomatic high-burden PVC (>25%) with RVOT origin and LV dilation. Exclusion: significant structural heart disease, sustained VT, contraindication to beta-blockers.
Postoperative shock with suspected ischemic hepatitis; seed 1664617079

72-year-old female with prosthetic mitral valve replacement; paravalvular leak causing hemolytic anemia; variation seed 2688452660; eligible for percutaneous PVL closure; baseline status post MV replacement.
Meets criteria for acute hypercalcemic crisis with pancreatitis; age >18; not pregnant; ICU admission required; synthetic data seed 2689240012.

Cardiovascular trajectory simulating chylopericardium secondary to thoracic duct injury during complicated thoracic aortic aneurysm repair. Not a trial; standard postoperative cardiovascular complication progression.
HFpEF with preserved EF; exercise intolerance; severe chronotropic incompetence suspected; stable on diuretic therapy
ICU admission for neuroleptic malignant syndrome secondary to high-potency antipsychotics (haloperidol); no pregnancy; excludes age under 18 and known malignant hyperthermia susceptibility.
Inclusion: Advanced pancreatic head adenocarcinoma with biliary obstruction; Exclusion: Active systemic infection not addressed; Not enrolled in another trial.
Acute purulent pericarditis with pneumococcal empyema; 2-4 encounters; no major contraindications to IV antibiotics.

Male, 62 with ischemic cardiomyopathy presenting with sustained VT storm; candidate for acute invasive electrophysiology therapy; no contraindication to catheter ablation identified; consent obtained.
Simulated trial profile for acute type B aortic dissection with medical management using IV esmolol; Variation seed 245575459

Baseline diagnosis of non-muscle invasive bladder cancer (carcinoma in situ) refractory to intravesical BCG therapy; suitable candidate for definitive management via radical cystectomy with ileal conduit.
Eligible for standard surgical management of suspected ovarian germ cell tumor; no prior chemotherapy or radiotherapy; not enrolled in experimental trials.
Meets age criteria for oncology workup; presenting with Horner's syndrome and ulnar neuropathic pain consistent with Pancoast tumor; no prior thoracic cancer; able to consent.

Male, age 60-75 with metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer and comorbidity pancytopenia due to diffuse bone marrow infiltration; currently not a candidate for high-dose myelosuppressive chemotherapy; requires hematology-oncology management.

Not enrolled in a clinical trial; presents with duodenal adenocarcinoma causing recurrent upper GI bleeding and profound malnutrition; candidate for multidisciplinary palliative nutrition and oncology evaluation.
AML with induction chemotherapy; presents with neutropenic fever, septic shock; suspected fungal pneumonia; eligible for ICU sepsis management
Age 40-60 with cryptogenic ischemic stroke and suspected paradoxical embolism via patent foramen ovale (PFO); bubble study positive for PFO; candidate for transcatheter PFO closure.

Eligible for LVAD vs transplant workup; no active systemic infection; BMI ~28; preserved organ function adequate for device/organ transplant consideration; consent obtained for advanced heart failure therapies workup.
Female, 18-50, May-Thurner syndrome with acute left iliac vein DVT; no active bleeding; able to receive anticoagulation; hemodynamically stable.
Takotsubo syndrome candidate; postmenopausal female with acute chest pain; ACS ruled out by coronary angiography; compatible with stress cardiomyopathy feasibility criteria.
Eligible for percutaneous balloon mitral valvuloplasty; rheumatic mitral stenosis with mitral valve area ≤1.5 cm2; no significant left atrial thrombus on TEE; adequate comorbidity profile for PBMV.
Inclusion: ECOG 1-2; adequate hepatic and renal function; not enrolled in another trial. Exclusion: active infection, uncontrolled comorbidity, prior platinum intolerance.
Inclusion: adult female with acute hypoglycemia and suspected endogenous hyperinsulinism; Exclusion: pregnancy, hepatic failure, uncorrected electrolyte disturbances.

Inclusion: acute iliofemoral DVT with phlegmasia cerulea dolens; Exclusion: active major bleeding, recent intracranial hemorrhage, platelet count <50,000/µL, severe coagulopathy, uncontrolled hypertension; consent obtained.
HIV-positive patient presenting with cognitive decline; imaging and CSF studies compatible with primary CNS lymphoma; able to tolerate HD-MTX-based therapy with ART support.
Inclusion: adult (≥18) with septic shock due to healthcare-associated pneumonia; Exclusion: enrollment in another interventional trial; pregnancy not applicable; no preexisting DNR.
Surgical candidacy confirmed for severe aortic regurgitation due to ascending aortic aneurysm; has acceptable renal function and no active infection or contraindication to cardiothoracic surgery.
Inclusion: Metastatic androgen-secreting testicular tumor with virilization; Exclusion: Contraindication to GnRH agonist therapy or planned orchiectomy
AML induction chemotherapy candidate; no active secondary malignancies; informed consent obtained; not enrolled in conflicting trials.
Stage III HER2-positive breast cancer; planned anthracycline + trastuzumab neoadjuvant therapy; baseline LVEF adequate; no contraindications to HER2 therapy.

Eligible: diagnosed with Chagas cardiomyopathy with advanced apical LV aneurysm and chronic thromboembolic disease; age 55-65; no contraindication to ACE inhibitors or anticoagulation; able to comply with follow-up.
Inclusion: Adult female with newly diagnosed anterior mediastinal mass and clinically confirmed myasthenia gravis; Exclusion: pregnancy, prior chemotherapy for thymic malignancy.

Meets criteria for high-risk venous thromboembolism with limb-threatening phlegmasia cerulea dolens; no contraindication identified to catheter-directed therapy or systemic anticoagulation after multidisciplinary review.

65-year-old male with suspected left main coronary artery equivalent disease presenting with chest pain and ACS risk; severe stenosis in proximal LAD and proximal LCx identified on angiography; eligible for percutaneous coronary intervention with bifurcation stenting and dual antiplatelet therapy; no contraindications to high-intensity statin or ACE inhibitor.
Eligibility: age 50-75, ECOG 0-1; histologically confirmed gastric MALT lymphoma with Helicobacter pylori infection; adequate organ function; no prior systemic lymphoma therapy within 6 months.
Acute inferior STEMI with Mobitz II AV block; requires temporary pacing and urgent RCA revascularization; conduction abnormality managed with pacing and close rhythm monitoring.
2-4 encounters; cardiovascular carcinoid heart disease trajectory; baseline with severe tricuspid stenosis; octreotide therapy ongoing; diuresis; plan for valve intervention.

58-year-old male with chronic alcohol use presenting with acute decompensated heart failure and global hypokinesis; echocardiography shows LVEF around 25%. No prior MI documented; suitable for guideline-directed medical therapy titration as stabilization occurs.

Inclusion: 65-75-year-old female with newly diagnosed multiple myeloma presenting with hypercalcemia (Ca > 11 mg/dL), acute kidney injury (Cr > 1.5 mg/dL), and radiographically confirmed pathologic vertebral fracture; Exclusion: prior MM-directed therapy, uncontrolled infection, or history of stem cell transplant.

Female, age 54, Paget's disease of nipple with underlying invasive ductal carcinoma; candidate for breast-conserving surgery with sentinel lymph node biopsy; no prior breast cancer; seed 3545254502 used for trajectory generation.

Inclusion: Female patient aged 60+ with symptomatic subclavian steal syndrome causing vertebrobasilar insufficiency during left upper-extremity exertion. Exclusion: None contraindicated for endovascular intervention.
Seed 94654079: Meets criteria for thoracic malignancy with prior radiotherapy; dysphagia due to radiation-induced esophagitis; ECOG 1; not enrolled in conflicting trials.
Hypoxic-ischemic brain injury after prolonged out-of-hospital cardiac arrest; undergoing targeted temperature management protocol.
Inclusion: ICU-level care for acute toxic shock syndrome with Staphylococcus aureus wound infection; Exclusion: none documented.
Acute type A aortic dissection involving ascending aorta with innominate artery involvement; urgent surgical candidate; no contraindication to emergent repair
Eligible: post-CABG patient with suspected chylopericardium due to thoracic duct injury; no pregnancy; no known allergy to octreotide or contrast agents; has consented to study data generation.
ICU admission for hypovolemic shock due to torrential secretory diarrhea with electrolyte depletion; adult patient; no contraindications to aggressive fluid resuscitation.

Meets criteria for severe aplastic anemia: ANC <0.5 x10^9/L, platelets <20 x10^9/L, hypocellular marrow. Eligible for immunosuppressive therapy with ATG and cyclosporine. Transplant evaluation ongoing.
Eligible: age >18 with prosthetic mitral valve; no contraindication to anticoagulation; stable hemodynamics; planned medical management with anticoagulation bridging.

Seed: 3053951383. Meets criteria for ICU endocrine emergency simulation with myxedema coma requiring mechanical ventilation and vasopressor support. Excludes pregnancy, known terminal illness with DNR, or advanced directive precluding ICU escalation.
Adult Kawasaki disease with documented bilateral coronary artery aneurysms; trajectory seeded with 118071017.
Not enrolled in any clinical trial; age 50-65; ICU admission for severe accidental hypothermia with ventricular fibrillation; candidate for internal ECMO rewarming with ECPR protocol.
Inclusion: Adult with Doege-Potter syndrome due to IGF-2 producing solitary fibrous tumor of pleura; Exclusion: poor surgical candidate

Diagnosis consistent with chronic thromboembolic pulmonary hypertension (CTEPH); right heart failure due to CTEPH; planned medical optimization with diuretics and riociguat; not a candidate for immediate surgical pulmonary endarterectomy due to distal disease; requires close follow-up with potential BPA evaluation
STEMI inferior wall; eligible for primary PCI; not enrolled in any other interventional trials.

Clinical trajectory generated for GI neuroendocrine tumor with diarrhea; criteria: adult female with metastatic GI NET; refractory diarrhea requiring octreotide therapy; no contraindication to somatostatin analogs.
Adult female with severe burn injury: 60% total body surface area (TBSA) burned, presenting with burn shock requiring ICU-level resuscitation and airway protection.

Meets ICU admission criteria for acute encephalopathy with suspected thiamine deficiency; not enrolled in any clinical trial; presents with ophthalmoplegia, ataxia, confusion consistent with Wernicke's encephalopathy due to malnutrition/alcohol risk.
Not enrolled in any trial; presenting with severe heat stroke requiring ICU-level cooling and supportive care.
Seed 2839803934: ICU/Critical Care trajectory for spinal epidural abscess with progressive paraplegia; baseline eligibility met for aggressive infection control and surgical decompression.
Seed 1032002960: Oncologic airway tumor case; tracheal adenoid cystic carcinoma simulation; feasible for airway-directed interventions with plan for radiotherapy and potential surgical resection.

Male patient, age 65, ECOG 2, with two concurrent advanced cancers (Renal cell carcinoma on immune checkpoint inhibitor and Stage IV NSCLC with brain metastases) and active immune-related adverse events (colitis and myocarditis). Not candidate for curative therapy; requires hold of IO therapy and planned palliative management.

Male, age 65-75 with infrarenal abdominal aortic aneurysm measuring approximately 6.0 cm, scheduled for elective EVAR after vascular surgical clearance. No contraindication to contrast or general anesthesia identified; adequate renal function and no severe comorbidity impacting operative risk.
Female, age 40-60; confirmed triple-negative breast cancer with chest wall recurrence; ECOG 0-1; not pregnant; adequate organ function; no prior platinum-based chemo within 4 weeks.
Meets cardiovascular risk criteria for Loeys-Dietz syndrome with aortic root dilation and ischemic cardiomyopathy with LVEF ≤ 35%. Not pregnant. Not enrolled in other interventional trials.

Ischemic cardiomyopathy with severe mitral regurgitation; NYHA class IV symptoms; candidate for guideline-directed medical therapy optimization and assessment for coronary revascularization; not enrolled in a clinical trial.
Not enrolled in any trial due to acute rupture with hemodynamic instability at presentation; qualifies for emergency oncologic management outside trial.
Baseline eligibility: 72-year-old female with COPD presenting with Multifocal atrial tachycardia (MAT) and severe acute hypercapnic respiratory failure; excludes pregnancy and age < 18.

Adult patient with suspected anomalous origin of left coronary artery from the pulmonary artery (ALCAPA); eligible for surgical repair; no contraindication to iodinated contrast; baseline stable enough for CTCA scheduling.
Not enrolled in a trial; presenting with CML in blast crisis and leukostasis; plan includes cytoreduction, TKI therapy, and transplant evaluation.

Inclusion: Adults ≥18 with metastatic cutaneous melanoma; confirmed BRAF V600 mutation; currently on combination BRAF/MEK inhibitor therapy; ECOG 0-1. Exclusion: Active infection requiring antibiotics, uncontrolled comorbidity, significant hepatic impairment.

Postmenopausal female with Paget's disease of the nipple and underlying invasive ductal carcinoma of the right breast; no known distant metastases on staging imaging; eligible for neoadjuvant chemotherapy followed by surgical resection and adjuvant endocrine therapy.

Stage III HER2-positive breast cancer; candidate for neoadjuvant AC followed by taxane and trastuzumab; developed anthracycline/trastuzumab-induced cardiotoxicity, limiting use of HER2-targeted therapy.
Met criteria for acute MI with post-infarction ventricular septal defect requiring surgical repair; 3-encounter trajectory.
Meets general oncology criteria; presenting with hypertrophic osteoarthropathy (HOA) in the setting of new lung adenocarcinoma; suitable for systemic therapy with non-squamous NSCLC and HOA syndrome.
Meets criteria for first-line targeted therapy with sunitinib for metastatic RCC; baseline organ function adequate; no prior systemic therapy for RCC.

Eligible: young male smoker (<40) with distal digitale ischemia and toe ulcers consistent with thromboangiitis obliterans; no diabetes; no widespread atherosclerotic disease; excludes significant renal/hepatic impairment, pregnancy, or alternative vasculitis.
Inclusion: age 60-75 with newly diagnosed multiple myeloma and hypercalcemia; Exclusion: active uncontrolled infection, pregnancy, prior MM therapy.
Meets criteria for oncology registry; bladder cancer with bilateral ureteral obstruction; not currently enrolled in a therapeutic trial.
Gastric MALT lymphoma with refractory Helicobacter pylori infection; eligible for rituximab-based therapy; baseline evaluation completed; no prior systemic lymphoma-directed therapy.
